english: Schr. and Wts. (where śi-mi (shi mi) stands), gen. źim-bu (zhim bu) Glr., or źum-bu (zhum bu) cat, C.
1. = ćig (cig). — 2. v. °jig-pa (hjig pa) I., ?, źig-rál-ba (zhig ral ba) demolished, ruined Mil.
(sbst. or adj.?) dense throng, or crowded together in a mass W.

I. sbst. क्षेत्र (Cs.: źiṅ-ma (zhing ma), źiṅ-po (zhing po), źiṅ-bu (zhing bu), perh. provincialisms), 1. field, ground, soil, arable land; t'áṅ-źiṅ (thang zhing) fields in a plain, level land, ri-źiṅ (ri zhing) fields on a mountain, hill-land; t'úl-źiṅ (thul zhing) W. (ni f.) cultivated land; źiṅ-k'a (zhing kha) = źiṅ (zhing), źiṅ-k'ai bú-mo (zhing khahi bu mo) the girls in the field Mil.; źiṅ rmó-ba (zhing rmo ba) frq., to plough a field; to carry on agriculture; °débs-pa (hdebs pa) to till, to sow a field, mi gcíg-gis btáb-pai (mi gcig gis btab pahi) a field that has been sowed by one man Glr.; źiṅ °c'ú-ba (zhing hchu ba) to irrigate a field (?) Cs.; rṅá-ba (rnga ba) to mow, to reap, a field, źiṅ-mk'an (zhing mkhan) reaper; *źiṅ bád-će* (zhing bad ce) W. to pursue husbandry; źiṅ bgód-pa (zhing bgod pa) to divide or distribute land Cs. — 2. fig., cf. źiṅ-k'ams (zhing khams), bsód-nams-kyi źiṅ (bsod nams kyi zhing) daṅ (dang) °prád-pa (hphrad pa) to enter the field of merit, to turn into the path of virtue Dzl.; °dúl-byai źiṅ-du gzígs-te (hdul byahi zhing du gzigs te) Pth. seeing him in the land of conversion (yet v. also 3, a.); region, źiṅ bću (zhing bcu) (Sch.: 'the ten regions') is said to signify something like: the reign of Evil. — 3. equivalent to saṅs-rgyás-kyi źiṅ (sangs rgyas kyi zhing) the kingdom of Buddha, a. in an earthly sense: a holy land, a land of salvation, where Buddha resides, or at least where Buddhism prevails; so also °dúl-bai źiṅ (hdul bahi zhing) land of conversion Glr.; acc. to Wts. it is a name of the earthly seat of Buddha, the residence of the Dalai Lama at Lhasa; b. supernaturally: heaven, paradise, Elysium i.e. one of the heavens inhabited by the Buddhist gods, or also the state of non-existence, Nirwāna; źiṅ-la péb-pa (zhing la peb pa) = bdé-bar gśégs-pa (bde bar gshegs pa) to die. — 4. body, v. źiṅ-c'én (zhing chen), źiṅ-lpágs (zhing lpags). — Comp and deriv. źiṅ-bkód (zhing bkod) map C., W.. źiṅ-gi bkód-pa (zhing gi bkod pa) v. Asiat. Res. XX., 425. — źiṅ-k'aṅ (zhing khang) 1. summer-house, pleasure-house, pavilion W. 2. field and house, the whole estate or property W. (= yul-gzís (yul gzhis)) — źiṅ-k'áms (zhing khams) = źiṅ (zhing) 2 and 3, frq. — źiṅ-k'rúṅs (zhing khrungs), źiṅ-gi k'rúṅs-pa (zhing gi khrungs pa) or -ma (ma) the produce of the field Cs. — źiṅ-k'ród (zhing khrod) many fields together Cs. — źiṅ-rgód (zhing rgod) rough, uncultivated ground Sch. — źiṅ-c'én (zhing chen) and -c'úṅ (-chung) a large and a small field; also: a large and a small body or corpse Thgr. — źiṅ-mc'óg (zhing mchog) paradise, a most delightful country, an Eden, an Eldorado Pth. — źiṅ-bdág (zhing bdag) proprietor of a field, land-owner. — źiṅ-pa (zhing pa) husbandman, farmer Dzl. — źiṅ-lpágs (zhing lpags) a skin (pulled off), hide. — źiṅ-mu (zhing mu) boundary of a field, landmark. — źiṅ-bzáṅ (zhing bzang) good land, productive soil Cs. — źiṅ-gśin (zhing gshin) dead, arid, burnt soil Cs. — źiṅ-sa (zhing sa) 1. ground, soil, arable land Cs. 2. province Sch. II. gerundial termin. = ćiṅ (cing), q.v.
resp. fine flour, also flour in general, źib-k'úṅ bag (zhib khung bag), źib-pór (zhib por) box, for flour Cs.

B., źib-po (zhib po) Cs., źib-mo (zhib mo) C., W. 1. fine, of powder and similar things, źib-rtsíṅ (zhib rtsing) fine and coarse Zam.; źib-par byéd-pa (zhib par byed pa), B.* źib-mo ćó-će* (zhib mo co ce) W., to make fine, to pound, to reduce to powder. — 2. accurate, exact, strict, precise, ltá-ba yáṅs-śiṅ spyód-lam źib-par mdzéd (lta ba yangs shing spyod lam zhib par mdzed) be wide in your views, but strict in your actions Glr.; so Sch. understands also źib-źib yod (zhib zhib yod), źib-po med (zhib po med), źib-rgyu med (zhib rgyu med), which ought however to be translated: 'I have accurate information, I have no precise information, I have no particulars to communicate'; źib-mo śés-pa (zhib mo shes pa) to know accurately; more frq adv. źib-par (zhib par), źib-tu (zhib tu) B., *źib-c'a (zhib cha) *vulg., exactly, precisely, thoroughly źib-tu gsól-ba (zhib tu gsol ba), gtam źib-tu byéd-pa (gtam zhib tu byed pa) to report accurately Dzl. (the former resp.); źib-par bśád-pa (zhib par bshad pa) Glr. id.; źib-par (śes-) °dód-na (zhib par (shes) hdod na) if you wish to know it accurately Glr.; bka źib-tu bgros-pa (bka zhib tu bgros pa) resp., to consult carefully Pth; bka-mc'íd gsuṅ-glén źib-tu bgyíd (bka mchid gsung glen zhib tu bgyid) gentlemen, discourse as freely as you please! Mil.; las-rgyu-°bras źib-tu mi rtsi-na (las rgyu hbras zhib tu mi rtsi na) if one does not strictly regard the doctrine of retaliation Mil.; *źib-c'a ltos (zhib cha ltos) (or to*) W. look at it well, carefully; *źib-c'a zer* (zhib cha zer) W. pronounce it accurately; *źib-c'a ćó-pa* (zhib cha co pa) C. to examine closely; *źib-sál* (zhib sal) W. accurately and distinctly.
n. of a medicinal herb Med.
gen. źim-po (zhim po), well-tasted, sweet-scented, *źim-po rag* (zhim po rag) W. I find the
